ddls:
CREATE TABLE "players" (
  "player_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each player */,
  "player_name" TEXT /* Name of the player */,
  "ally_code" TEXT /* Ally code used to identify the player */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the player */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the player's account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the player in the game */,
  "starting_gear_needed" INTEGER /* Amount of starting gear needed by the player */,
  "progress" INTEGER /* Current progress of the player towards gear acquisition */,
  "current_gear_needed" INTEGER /* Amount of current gear still needed by the player */,
  "gp" INTEGER /* Gold Points earned by the player */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the player account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the player account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  "leader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team leader */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the team was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the team was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teams_leader_id FOREIGN KEY ("leader_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_teams" (
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the player belongs to */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the player within the team */,
  "joined_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the player joined the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("player_id", "team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gear_requirements" (
  "gear_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gear requirement */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player associated with the gear requirement */,
  "gear_type" TEXT /* Type of gear required (e.g., weapon, armor) */,
  "required_amount" INTEGER /* Amount of gear required */,
  "gear_level" INTEGER /* Level of the gear required */,
  "gear_rarity" TEXT /* Rarity of the gear required */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gear_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gear_requirements_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "progress_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player related to the progress update */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the update was made */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the progress made */,
  "progress_type" TEXT /* Type of progress made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_progress_updates_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gold_points_transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player making the transaction */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., earned, spent) */,
  "amount" INTEGER /* Amount of gold points involved in the transaction */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the transaction occurred */,
  "transaction_description" TEXT /* Description of the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gold_points_transactions_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "player_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player associated with the setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., notification preferences) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "notification_preferences" TEXT /* Notification preferences of the player */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_player_settings_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "player_id" INTEGER /* ID of the player associated with the character */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "character_type" TEXT /* Type of the character */,
  "character_level" INTEGER /* Level of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_characters_player_id FOREIGN KEY ("player_id") REFERENCES players ("player_id")
)
